Dashboard Archive: 2026-06-04 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker-long dashboard shows two exceptional performers with very different risk profiles. CLMT ranks #1 with a score of 92.7, displaying consistent acceleration across timeframes (3.86% 1m to 956.78% 10y) and passing all five momentum ratio thresholds while trading above all major moving averages. EACO appears as the second entry with even more extreme 10-year returns at 1955.89% versus SPY's outperformance of 1694.13%, though its shorter-term ratios are more compressed (2.33-4.17 range), suggesting a bumpier path to those gains. Both stocks show complete SMA alignment and strong positioning, but CLMT's smoother progression ratio and higher 5-year relative performance (+458.92% vs SPY) indicate more sustainable momentum, while EACO's massive 10-year spike appears driven by recovery or structural revaluation rather than consistent acceleration. The negative correlation of both names to tech (QQQ: -0.173 and -0.141 respectively) suggests these are non-tech secular winners, likely benefiting from commodity or alternative asset inflation.
